questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Shore Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple stands at Mamallapuram (Mahabalipuram) in the Chengalpattu district of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: On the shore of which body of water does the Shore Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The temple sits on the coast of the Bay of Bengal, which gives it its name.

questions.q: The Shore Temple is part of the famous monuments at which town?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple is one of the Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram).

questions.q: Which dynasty built the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple was built by the Pallava dynasty, who ruled this part of South India.

questions.q: The Shore Temple is primarily dedicated to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: Its main shrines are dedicated to Shiva, with an additional shrine to Vishnu.

questions.q: What international recognition does the Shore Temple hold as part of Mahabalipuram?
questions.explanation: The Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram, including the Shore Temple, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: Roughly in which century was the Shore Temple built?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple was built in the early 8th century CE, making it one of South India's oldest stone temples.

questions.q: The Shore Temple is notable for being one of the oldest examples in South India of temples built from what?
questions.explanation: It is among the earliest structural (cut-stone) temples in South India, unlike the region's earlier rock-cut shrines.

questions.q: What is the most distinctive feature crowning the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is crowned by tall, tapering pyramidal towers known as vimanas in Dravidian style.

questions.q: Mahabalipuram, where the Shore Temple stands, was a major what for the Pallavas?
questions.explanation: Mahabalipuram was an important Pallava seaport on the Coromandel Coast.

questions.q: Which Pallava king is traditionally credited with building the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple is generally attributed to Narasimhavarman II, also known as Rajasimha.

questions.q: From what type of stone is the Shore Temple chiefly built?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple is constructed mainly of locally quarried granite blocks.

questions.q: The Shore Temple complex is unusual because it houses shrines to which two deities together?
questions.explanation: It contains two Shiva shrines facing east and west and a shrine to the reclining Vishnu (Anantashayana) between them.

questions.q: What is the architectural style of the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: The Shore Temple is an early and influential example of the Dravidian style of South Indian temple architecture.

questions.q: The reclining Vishnu image at the Shore Temple is shown in which famous posture?
questions.explanation: A pre-existing rock-cut sculpture shows Vishnu as Anantashayana, reclining on the cosmic serpent Ananta.

questions.q: Rows of which sculpted animals top the boundary wall around the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple's enclosure wall is surmounted by rows of seated Nandi (bull) sculptures, the mount of Shiva.

questions.q: Which other site at Mahabalipuram is famous as a giant open-air rock relief?
questions.explanation: Mahabalipuram also features the massive open-air relief known as the Descent of the Ganga or Arjuna's Penance.

questions.q: What is the alternative spelling/name of the town that honours the Pallava king Narasimhavarman I 'Mamalla'?
questions.explanation: The town's name Mamallapuram derives from Narasimhavarman I's title 'Mamalla' (great wrestler).

questions.q: Why does the Shore Temple face the sea in a way that catches the first light?
options: Its main shrine is oriented to the east toward the rising sun
It faces north toward the Himalayas
It is oriented to the setting sun in the west
It deliberately faces away from sunlight
questions.explanation: The principal Shiva shrine faces east toward the Bay of Bengal, catching the rising sun's first rays.

questions.q: What major ongoing natural threat has long endangered the Shore Temple?
questions.explanation: Its seaside location exposes the granite to centuries of erosion from waves, salt spray, and wind.

questions.q: Which organisation is responsible for conserving and protecting the Shore Temple today?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India maintains the Shore Temple as a protected monument.

questions.q: The Shore Temple's small Vishnu shrine and twin Shiva towers make it an example of which kind of temple plan?
options: A multi-shrine complex within one enclosure
questions.explanation: It is a complex of three shrines—two Shiva vimanas and one Vishnu cell—within a single enclosure.

questions.q: The Pallava capital, from which the rulers who built the Shore Temple governed, was which inland city?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was the Pallava capital, while Mahabalipuram served as their coastal port.

questions.q: The Shore Temple is sometimes linked to a local legend about how many temples once standing on the coast?
questions.explanation: European sailors called the site the 'Seven Pagodas,' a legend of seven temples of which the Shore Temple is said to be the survivor.

questions.q: What is the tall, tapering tower over the sanctum of a Dravidian temple like the Shore Temple called?
questions.explanation: In Dravidian architecture the tower rising over the sanctum is the vimana; the gateway tower is the gopuram.

questions.q: The Shore Temple's design is considered a transition from earlier Pallava work of what kind?
options: Rock-cut cave temples and monolithic rathas
questions.explanation: It marks the move from the Pallavas' earlier rock-cut caves and monolithic rathas to fully structural stone temples.

questions.q: After the December 2004 Indian Ocean tsunami, the receding waters near the Shore Temple briefly revealed what?
options: Submerged rock structures and sculptures offshore
Ancient Roman coins on the beach
questions.explanation: The 2004 tsunami's receding sea briefly exposed offshore rock formations and carvings, fuelling the Seven Pagodas legend.

questions.q: Which title of the Pallava king Rajasimha is reflected in inscriptions associated with his temple-building?
questions.explanation: Narasimhavarman II's title Rajasimha is echoed in the name of the larger Shiva shrine, often called Rajasimheshvara.

questions.q: To shield the Shore Temple from the sea, conservators have notably planted and installed what along the shore?
options: Rows of casuarina trees and breakwaters
A glass enclosure around the temple
An artificial sand dune painted gold
questions.explanation: Casuarina plantations and rock breakwaters were added to reduce wind and wave erosion at the site.

questions.q: The Shore Temple's enclosure includes a small tank near a shrine that depicts which durga-related image?
options: A seated Durga with a lion
A standing Surya in his chariot
questions.explanation: Among the Shore Temple's carvings is an image of Durga seated with her lion mount, reflecting Pallava devotion to the goddess.
